mission.title: valkey: [NEW] Trigger manual failover on SIGTERM to primary (cluster)
mission.description: [NEW] Trigger manual failover on SIGTERM to primary (cluster). Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
**The problem/use-case that the feature addresses**

When a primary disappears, its slots are not served until an automatic failover happens. It takes about 3 seconds (node timeout plus some second). It's too much time for us to not accept writes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [NEW] Trigger manual failover on SIGTERM to primary…
steps.description:
## update

The `auto-failover-on-shutdown` was removed in #2292, please see #2292 for more details.

========================================

When a primary disappears, its slots are not served until an automatic
failover happens. It takes about n seconds (node timeout plus some seconds).
It's too much time for us to not accept writes.

If the host machine is about to shutdown for any reason, the processes
typically get a sigterm and have some time to shutdown gracefully. In
Kubernetes, this

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[NEW] Trigger manual failover on SIGTERM to primary…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## update

The `auto-failover-on-shutdown` was removed in #2292, please see #2292 for more details.

========================================

When a primary disappears, its slots are not served until an automatic
failover happens. It takes about n seconds (node timeout plus some seconds).
It's too much time for us to not accept writes.

prerequisites.description: A working valkey installation or development environment.
